title: Gate build-phase exit on `go vet ./acs/cycleN/...` compiling — catch tdd-seam drift (949's undefined WithJudgeModel) in-cycle instead of as an opaque audit FAIL
why: Cycle-949's builder implemented a different API shape than the tdd predicates encoded (never added core.WithJudgeModel), so the predicate package could not compile. Nothing checked predicate compilability until the ACS lane at audit time — 4 phases (test-amplification, coverage-gate, bug-reproduction, audit-narrative) ran and PASSed on work whose acceptance predicates were already un-runnable. A 2-second `go vet ./go/acs/cycleN/...` at build exit fails fast with the exact compiler error, in the phase that can still fix it.
how_to_apply: Per phases_are_config_only: add the check to the build phase deliverable contract / phase-gate precondition for build->next transition (Go orchestrator side, config-driven): if go/acs/cycle<N> exists in the worktree, `go vet ./go/acs/cycle<N>/...` must exit 0; non-zero => build-phase RETRY verdict with compiler stderr as the failure reason (fed back to builder). Do NOT run the predicates (that is the audit ACS lane's job) — compile-only.
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-949/cycle-state.json completed_phases (10 phases ran); retrospective-stdout.log compile root-cause
